items.title: Philippines urges ASEAN to strengthen crisis coordination amid Iran war
Middle East crisis live: Iran reviewing peace proposal as Trump says a deal ‘very possible’
items.description: The US and Iran have offered conflicting messages over the likelihood of a deal being reached imminentlyMorning and welcome to the Guardian’s continuing coverage of the crisis in the Middle East.The US and Iran have offered conflicting messages over the state of negotiations to end the war, with Donald Trump signalling…

items.title: Affected by the Iran war, residents in Southeast Asian countries voice their concerns
items.description: Southeast Asian countries are voicing concern about President Trump and the war with Iran.

items.title: The Gaza journalist killed on the day his daughter was born
items.description: Sana's first birthday is shadowed by loss; her father Yahya was killed in an Israeli attack hours after her birth.

items.title: Venice and Biennale 'shaken': 'The turmoil on the international scene has entered the exhibition'
items.description: Mark Owen is pleased to welcome Angelo Amante, Reuters correspondent in Rome. Russia has returned to the 2026 edition of the Venice Biennale for the first time since the full scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022. Their presence has triggered protests from activists, artists, and political dissidents who argue that culture…

items.title: After cancer revelation, court orders PM to submit sealed medical records in libel case
items.description: Israel says it coordinated delivery of rat pesticides to Gaza to counter infestation * IDF continues striking south Lebanon as Hezbollah targets troops with rockets, drones The post After cancer revelation, court orders PM to submit sealed medical records in libel case appeared first on The Times of Israel.

items.title: Fulani and Tuareg communities in Mali report abuses after jihadist offensive
items.description: The two communities have come under suspicion of collaborating with the JNIM jihadist group and the pro-independence Azawad Liberation Front, which launched attacks across the country on April 25. Several disappearances have been reported.

items.title: Ceasefire Violations, Nuclear Missile Tests, and Putin’s Coup Rumors – Ukraine Latest, May 6
items.description: Zelensky’s response to Russia’s ceasefire violations, Russia’s plan to test nuclear-capable missiles, and potential breakthroughs in US-Iran war – updates from Ukraine and beyond

items.title: Myanmar: What's behind Suu Kyi's transfer to house arrest?
items.description: The transfer of Aung San Suu Kyi from prison to house arrest has been welcomed cautiously abroad, but experts say Myanmar's military may be using it to boost legitimacy as the nation remains engulfed in civil war.

items.title: Robot wars - what an operation in Ukraine tells us about the battlefield of the near future
items.description: After Ukraine's President Zelensky said territory had been captured using just robots and drones, what is the future of unmanned warfare?

items.title: Zelensky, 'Mosca ha violato tregua, agiremo di conseguenza'
items.description: 'Ovvio che una guerra in corso non è il momento giusto per celebrazioni pubbliche'

items.title: UAE Accuses Iran Of Hitting Oil Sector, World Leaders Condemn Dangerous Escalation
items.description: Several countries have condemned Iran and called an attack on oil facilities in the United Arab Emirates a dangerous escalation. Thick smoke was filmed pouring out from the Fujairah Oil Industry Zone that the UAE claimed was hit by an Iranian drone attack on May 4. This comes as both the US and Iran fired shots in the …

items.title: How Colombia became a top exporter of mercenaries
items.description: Every year Colombia retires thousands of battle-hardened veterans from its armed forces. Many end up enlisting in foreign conflicts.

items.title: Education or food? Funding shortfalls force Sudanese refugees in Egypt to ‘make very difficult decisions’
items.description: Civilians who have fled the war in Sudan and sought shelter in neighbouring Egypt could potentially face a new battle – the loss of critical services that ensure their survival, the UN refugee agency, UNHCR, has warned.

items.title: U.K. Targets Russian Army Recruitment Network and Drone Suppliers in Latest Sanctions Package
items.description: The new measures target what British officials condemned as a “barbaric” network that lures foreign nationals to Russia with the promise of work, only to force them into frontline combat in Ukraine or military production.
